free_text: Large scale accelerator production of 225Ac: Effective
cross sections for 78 - 192 MeV protons incident on
232Th targets
Isotope Production Facility (IPF) uses
a 100-MeV proton beam extracted from the main LANSCE
linear accelerator. IPF was used to irradiate samples
at energies bellow 100 MeV
The Brookhaven Linac Isotope Producer
BLIP  consists of a beam line and target area for
isotope production that uses protons up to 200 MeV
energy and 110 mu-A intensity from the BNL Linac.
Canberra Model GC2020 High-Purity Germanium
detector with a relative efficiency of 20% was used
for radioactivity measurements
. Activation method was used followed by
chemical separation. Samples were irradiated for
period from 3 to 220 hours by average current ranging
from 30 mu-A (BLIP) to  200 mu-A (IPF)
For irradiations at IPF metallic Th was arc melted and
rolled to thickness 637.6 mg/cm2. The purity was better
than 99.6%. The discs were electron-beam welded into
machined Inconel capsules. Three different target were
used at IPF.
For the irradiations at BLIP, Th foils (0.125 mm,
99.5% purity) were purchased from Goodfellow
Corporation. For a typical irradiation, a 27.9 mm
circle was cut from the foil and wrapped in 0.025 mm
Al metal foil

Systematic uncertainty in beam intensity
Systematic uncertainty in sampling and
detector efficiency

propagation
